memory_storage_contents: detector-visible object states with source/evidence kind/confidence
support-surface candidates per frame and per target
object-id alias/provenance when detector label conflicts with simulator-style object id
relation hypotheses with evidence paths and confidence, not only final relation edges
memory_storage_method: append-only observation memory records before graph collapse
separate observed facts from inferred location hypotheses
store top-k candidate supports with abstain/ambiguity flags
retain frame-level visible-region anchors as evidence, but not as final semantic answers
query_contents: query target object by case id/object id/label plus episode and step
retrieve target state, visible-frame evidence, support candidates, room fallback, and missing-evidence status together
return why a support relation is chosen or why the system abstains
query_method: rank explicit detector current_location above inferred relation hypotheses
rank support candidates by same-frame evidence, label compatibility, distance, and ambiguity margin
fallback to room only after support candidate retrieval fails
emit structured missing-evidence blockers for detector recall handoff when query cannot answer

goal: VLM-only semantic success rate must be at least 50% before starting the next DSG experiment stage; DSG experiments then optimize memory contents, storage method, query contents, and query method.
next_actions: Use p31-detector-recall-handoff.json to obtain or synthesize external detector JSONL without gold answers.
Import returned detector JSONL into an observation sequence.
Build a memory-first predicted graph that stores observed facts and inferred support hypotheses separately.
Run P32 DSG candidate/eval only after the VLM semantic gate remains >= 50%.
Compare DSG against VLM P26 and record whether memory/query changes improve semantic match without increasing regressions.

vlm_gate.note: The 50% gate uses semantic location matching because strict JSON exact match is currently 0/60 for VLM-only and is tracked as a secondary normalization target.
